title: CRUD Operations: Creating, Reading, Updating, and Deleting Records Using Models

content.problemStatement:
When you were learning SQL - recall that you had learnt about CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) operations.

In this lesson, we'll explore how to perform CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) operations using models in Flask with SQLAlchemy. 

1. Creating Records:
To create a new record, you'll create a model object and add it to the database session:

```python
new_user = User(username='john_doe', email='john@example.com')
db.session.add(new_user)
db.session.commit()
```

2. Reading Records:
To retrieve records, you can use various query methods provided by SQLAlchemy:

```python
# Get all users
all_users = User.query.all()

# Get a specific user by ID
user = User.query.get(1)

# Filter users
filtered_users = User.query.filter_by(username='john_doe').first()
```
